fact: March 6 is exactly 65 days into the year, which means there are exactly 300 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 6 typically falls when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
March 6 sits right in the peak of 'kitten season' when outdoor cats often start having babies because of longer daylight!
The sun usually rises about 6 minutes earlier on March 6 than it did just one week before - that's like gaining a whole cartoon episode of extra morning light!
March 6 is a Pisces day in the zodiac, which means people born today are supposedly dreamy fish-people who love to swim in their own imagination!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 6 is when many spiders typically start getting ready for winter by spinning thicker, stronger webs!
March 6 falls during the time when earthworms often start wiggling up from deep underground because the soil is finally warming up enough in northern climates!
In cold places like Canada, March 6 is often prime time for 'sugar snow' - the perfect sticky snow that's amazing for building snowmen but terrible for snowball fights!
Frogs in ponds typically start making their loudest, most obnoxious croaking sounds around March 6 because they're basically screaming 'SPRING IS COMING!' to attract mates!
The daylight on March 6 lasts about 11 hours and 30 minutes in most temperate places, which means we're gaining roughly 3 minutes of extra sunlight every single day!

March 6 sits right in the middle of when deciduous trees (the ones that lose their leaves) start pumping water up from their roots so hard you can actually hear them gurgling if you listen closely in northern regions!
